text:
35. No such explanation has been offered by P.W. 7. The opinion of the High Court is based on the surmises and conjectures. We may, at this juncture, also notice the medical evidences brought on record. P.W. 9-Dr. Madan Mohan, performed the post-mortem examination. He conducted the post-mortem examination on 14 April 1992 both of Ram Gopal and Chatarvati. The death, according to him, took place on 1 day before the examination, which would take us about 10 p.m. on 12 April 1992. The ante-mortem injuries found on the body of Ram Gopal are already mentioned. He, in his evidence, stated :
"The direction of injury No. 1 of Ram Gopal was from upwards to downwards. The injury No. 1 is possible is somebody is lying and one fires from the side of head towards the legs from the top keeping his barrel parallel to the direction of body, from a distance. But then in that condition injuries No. 2 and 3 are not possible from one fire. There is a bleak possibility that Ram Gopal had received all the three injuries, from three different shots."
36. The direction and dispersal of injury sustained by Ram Gopal did not tally with the prosecution case, which, according to the learned Trial Judge, raises a doubt about the presence of the prosecution witnesses. The High Court, however, opined that the pellets were of small size and could be deflected easily and there is a possibility of it that pellets could change their direction after hitting them with a force. The said opinion was arrived at by the High Court on the premise that the dispersal of pellets, as mentioned in authoritative texts, were regular factory made cartridges. The High Court failed to notice that appellant No. 1 was said to have been carrying licensed double barrel gun and thus authoritative text as regard direction and dispersal of the injuries could be relied upon. The High Court, in this regard, opined as under :
"The dispersal of the pellets as mentioned in authoritative texts is with regard to regular factory made cartridges. Besides Budh Singh, the remaining five accused were carrying country made pistols and country made guns. It is quite likely that locally made or hand-filled cartridge had been used where the position of dispersal of pellets may be entirely different."
